title: Directives for standardization -- Part 1: Rules for the structure and drafting of standardizing documents

elements.description: Cover page containing the standard number, title, and classification information. Must include ICS code, CCS code, GB/T number, Chinese and English titles, and issue/implementation dates.
elements.notes: Required element. Standard number format: GB/T XXXXX-YYYY or GB XXXXX-YYYY. Title must be concise and unambiguous.

elements.description: Automatically generated list of: foreword, introduction, numbered clauses, annexes (with normative/informative label), bibliography, index, and lists of tables/figures.
elements.notes: Optional. When present, lists all headings in order with page numbers. Annex labels must include (normative) or (informative) designation.

elements.description: Required element providing administrative information about the standard. Must state the drafting rules standard (GB/T 1.1-2020), revision history, proposing organization, drafting organization(s), and responsible technical committee.
elements.notes: REQUIRED. Must cite 'GB/T 1.1-2020 Directives for standardization'. No scope, requirements, or informative annexes in foreword. Cannot contain tables or figures.

elements.description: Optional element providing background, reason for the standard, and relationship to other documents. Should explain why the standard was developed. No normative requirements. Subclauses numbered 0.1, 0.2, etc.
elements.notes: Optional. Cannot contain requirements. Numbered 0.1, 0.2... if subdivided. Patent declarations go here (see Annex D).

elements.description: ALWAYS Clause 1. Defines the subject matter and applicability of the standard. States what the standard specifies, establishes, gives, or provides. Short, precise statement of what the document covers.
elements.notes: REQUIRED. Always numbered Clause 1. Uses precise formulation: 'This standard specifies...' / 'This standard gives...' / 'This standard establishes...' Cannot contain requirements. Can have subclauses.

elements.description: Always Clause 2. The chapter heading is ALWAYS required (mandatory shell). The content (reference list) is conditional: present if normative references exist, absent otherwise. When absent, the placeholder 'This document has no normative references.' (GB/T original: 本文件没有规范性引用文件。) is inserted per section 8.6.2.
elements.standard_text_source: GB/T 1.1-2020 sections 6.2.2.3 + 8.6.2; Table 3 footnote: 章编号和标题的设置是必备的，要素内容的有无根据具体情况进行选择
elements.empty_text_zh: 本文件没有规范性引用文件。
elements.empty_text_en: This document has no normative references.
elements.notes: REQUIRED SHELL, CONDITIONAL CONTENT. Clause 2 heading always appears. If references exist, use the standard leading sentence (8.6.2). If none, use the placeholder sentence. Element_type is informative (资料性) per Table 3 of GB/T 1.1-2020. Dated references specify a specific edition; undated references apply to the latest edition.

elements.description: Always Clause 3. The chapter heading is ALWAYS required (mandatory shell). The content (term entries) is conditional: if terms are defined here, list them; if terms come from another standard, cite it; if no terms are needed, use the placeholder. Per section 6.2.2.3: Terms and Definitions is a 必备 (required) normative element; its content may be absent per 8.7.2.
elements.standard_text_source: GB/T 1.1-2020 sections 6.2.2.3 + 8.7.2; Table 3 footnote: 章编号和标题的设置是必备的，要素内容的有无根据具体情况进行选择
elements.empty_text_zh: 本文件没有需要界定的术语和定义。
elements.empty_text_en: For the purposes of this document, there are no terms and definitions that need to be defined.

intro_preambles.own_only: 下列术语和定义适用于本文件。
intro_preambles.own_only_en: For the purposes of this document, the following terms and definitions apply.
intro_preambles.external_only: ……界定的术语和定义适用于本文件。
intro_preambles.external_only_en: For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in [REF] apply.
intro_preambles.both: ……界定的以及下列术语和定义适用于本文件。
intro_preambles.both_en: For the purposes of this document, the terms and definitions given in [REF] and the following apply.

elements.notes: REQUIRED SHELL, CONDITIONAL CONTENT. Clause 3 heading always appears. Three cases for preamble per section 8.7.2: (1) own terms only: 'the following apply'; (2) external reference only: '[Ref]...apply'; (3) both: '[Ref]... and the following apply'. If no terms: placeholder 'No terms and definitions that need to be defined.' Three-way intro preamble per 8.7.2.

elements.description: Lists symbols and abbreviations used in the standard. Symbols listed with their definitions. Abbreviations listed with their expansions. Order: symbols first (in order of appearance or alphabetically), then abbreviations alphabetically.
elements.notes: Optional. Usually Clause 4, but number depends on whether Terms and Definitions is present. If only symbols: title is 'Symbols'; if only abbreviations: title is 'Abbreviations'; if both: 'Symbols and Abbreviations'.

elements.name_zh: 技术要素（要求、试验方法等）
elements.description: The main technical content. Typically includes: General requirements, Specific requirements organized by topic, Test methods, Classification/marking/packaging. Clauses numbered 4 (or 5) onwards. Each major technical area is a separate numbered clause.
elements.notes: REQUIRED. At least one technical clause must be present. Clause numbering starts after symbols (typically Clause 4 or 5). Use 'shall' for requirements, 'should' for recommendations, 'may' for permissions, 'can' for possibilities.

elements.description: Normative annexes contain provisions that are part of the standard. Labeled Annex A, Annex B, etc. Title page states '(normative)'. Clauses within annex numbered A.1, A.2, B.1, B.2, etc.
elements.notes: Optional. Normative annexes form an integral part of the standard. Must be labeled '(normative)' after the annex heading. Numbered A, B, C... (Latin capital letters, not I, O).

elements.description: Informative annexes provide supplementary information that is not part of the standard requirements. Must be labeled '(informative)'. Lettered continuing from normative annexes.
elements.notes: Optional. Must be labeled '(informative)'. Cannot contain requirements (no 'shall' statements). Numbered continuing from normative annexes.

elements.description: Informative list of documents cited informatively or referenced for background. Not part of the normative content. Documents listed in the same order as normative references: national, industry, foreign, ISO/IEC.
elements.notes: Optional. Informative element. Documents in bibliography are NOT normative references. Uses GB/T 7714 citation format.

elements.description: Alphabetical index of terms, concepts, or subjects in the standard with clause references.
elements.notes: Optional. Informative element. Usually only included in large standards with extensive terminology.

element_ordering_rules.required_shell_note: Chapter heading is always present (必备 shell). Content is conditional per 8.6.2 and 8.7.2. Empty placeholder text required when content is absent. Source: GB/T 1.1-2020 6.2.2.3 + Table 3 footnote.

numbering_rules.annexes_labeled: A, B, C, ... (no I, O, X, Z reserved meanings)
